title: Puerto Banus Jet Ski Rental: Guide to Costs, Licences and Best Routes
html:
Jet ski rental in Puerto Banus offers a thrilling way to explore the Costa del Sol coastline, with rates typically ranging from €120 for a 30-minute session to €350 for a full hour on the Mediterranean. Departing from the harbour at Puerto Deportivo, you can access open water within minutes. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators for a seamless booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Puerto Banus?

Jet ski rental in Puerto Banus starts at €120 for 30 minutes and rises to €350 for a full hour on the Mediterranean. Prices vary by season and whether you choose a guided tour or solo ride. Most operators offer discounts for booking online through platforms such as boat rental Puerto Banus listings, where you can compare verified providers and secure your slot in advance.

Do you need a licence to rent a jet ski in Puerto Banus?

No licence is required for jet ski rental in Puerto Banus if the engine power is below 110hp, which covers most hire models. The operator will provide a mandatory 20-minute safety briefing. For higher-powered craft, a valid Spanish recreational boating licence or an International Certificate of Competence may be needed; the operator will confirm exact requirements at booking.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most jet ski rentals depart from Puerto Deportivo, the main sports harbour just west of the Puerto Banus marina. The nearby breakwater offers a calm area for beginners. From there you can head east to the beach of San Pedro de Alcántara or west past Puerto Banus itself towards the Marbella coastline. For longer trips, operators often include a stop at Cabo Pino lighthouse, about 3 nautical miles away. The exclusive residential area of La Zagaleta is visible from the water on clear days. Beyond that, the island of Alborán lies 50 nautical miles offshore but is only attempted on organised excursions.

Which boat to rent

If you prefer a private boat charter over a jet ski, consider these options available through BoatHire24. For example, the Olympic 490 listing shows a 5-metre speedboat ideal for a couple or small family. Here are four popular choices:

  1. Jet ski (solo), best for thrill-seekers, 1 person, from €120 for 30 minutes.
  2. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), ideal for coastal cruising, half-day from €250 (excluding fuel).
  3. Open speedboat (6-8 guests), suitable for watersports and short island hops, from €400 for 4 hours.
  4. Day cruiser (8-10 guests), offers cabin space and a toilet, full-day from €800.

Costs of jet ski rental in Puerto Banus: a breakdown

Here is a typical price band for jet ski and small boat hire in Puerto Banus:

  • Shared jet ski tour (30 min): €120 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from €250 (self-drive, no licence needed for ≤110hp).
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from €1,500 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed yacht (6 hours): from €2,500 for up to 8 guests, including skipper and fuel.

Season and licence tips

Jet ski rental is available year-round, but the highest season runs from June to September when the Levante wind can make the sea choppy; always check the forecast before booking.

For licence requirements, the guiding principle in Spain is that boats under 6 metres with an engine below 40kW (54hp) require no licence. However, most jet ski hire models fall under the 110hp threshold and are exempt. Always confirm with the operator. The summer months of July and August are the busiest, so booking ahead is advisable.

At a glance

  • Price range: €120, €350 per jet ski session
  • Peak season: June, September
  • Trip duration: 30 minutes to full day
  • Capacity: 1, 2 persons per jet ski
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about jet ski rental in Puerto Banus

What is the minimum age to ride a jet ski in Puerto Banus?

The minimum age to drive a jet ski in Spain is 16 years, with parental consent required for under-18s; passengers can be as young as 6.

Can I rent a jet ski without a licence in Puerto Banus?

Yes, you can rent a jet ski without a licence provided the engine power does not exceed 110hp, which covers all standard hire models.

Is it safe to jet ski in Puerto Banus?

Yes, operators provide life jackets, a safety briefing, and a designated riding area away from bathing zones and main shipping lanes.

What is the best time of day for jet skiing in Puerto Banus?

Early morning (9, 11am) offers calm seas and fewer boats, while late afternoon (5, 7pm) provides beautiful light for photos near the coast.

Whether you choose a jet ski or a private boat, BoatHire24 makes it simple to organise your day on the water. If you own a vessel in this area, consider listing it on our platform to reach thousands of potential renters worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
